LAW AND MOTION 301, MOTION NOTICE OF DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT; MOVING AND OPPOSING PARTIES APPEARED. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING -- DEFENDANT DOV DORZIA''S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NOTICE IS DEFECTIVE BECAUSE DEMANDS ARE MADE ON TENANT TO MAKE REPAIRS WHICH ARE NOT REQUIRED BY THE COVENANT REFERENCES IN THE NOTICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. HONORABLE A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II REPORTER: LEE BATARA, CSR 3759

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-03-2002 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant DOV DORZIA's demurrer -- Calendaring error. Case is dismissed. (JJM) Reporter: Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-26-2002 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant DOV DORZIA's demurrer is O F F C A L E N D A R -- this matter was heard and ruled on June 19, 2002 pursuant to an ex-parte order advancing hearing date. (JJM) Reporter: Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855

LAW AND MOTION
Off Calendar Jun-19-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Deborah Henderson, Esq. 552-2847 for defendants DOV DORZIA and LEVANA DORZIA demurring parties
Drexel A. Bradshaw, Esq. 433-4800 for Plaintiff HENRY LAM opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- Defendant DOV DORZIA's demurrer to Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Notice is defective because demands are made on tenant to make repairs which are not required by the covenant references in the notice. Order signed in open court. (AJR/kh) Lee Batara, CSR 3759
